question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. The Consumer Price Index increased by only 0.3% in March 2026, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for a YES resolution. Since 0.3% is less than 0.8%, the market definitively resolves to NO.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased 0.3% monthly
0.3% is well below the 0.8% threshold for YES resolution
Data was released April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET as scheduled
Market closes April 10, 2026 at 12:25 PM ET, after data release
risks: Potential data revision risk, though monthly CPI revisions are rare
Market price suggests traders may not be aware of actual results yet
